title: From The Whole To A Part
study: A Study In The Life Of John The
scriptureForWeek: VARIOUS PASSAGES IN THE GOSPELS Make sure you focus on the verses for each day, as the core concepts of John’s ministry really come into focus this week. He now begins to fade into the background as Jesus comes into focus. This is the heart of the shift that boys need to understand in order to become godly men.
weekTheme: Boys point to themselves; Men point to Jesus God will make you a witness THERE IS A WAR TODAY FOR YOUR ATTENTION. BOYS WANT ATTENTION FOR THEMSELVES, MEN USE IT TO POINT TO JESUS. What do you think is the most important asset that you possess as a teenager? It’s not money; you probably don’t have a ton of that at this point. It's not strength, as you still have some growing to do. It's not brains; you are going to learn a lot more in the days to come. Want to know what your most important asset is? It’s your attention. You are growing up in what has been described as an attention economy. There is a huge war going on behind the scenes to get you to pay attention to the things that other people want. The reason your attention is important is because what you pay attention to determines your reality. In today’s culture, everyone wants attention for themselves. Most people today long to be famous. Maybe not on a national level, but certainly among their friends. But wanting attention and paying attention are two different things. A mark of immaturity and insecurity is the need to always be the center of attention. The world can distort our perception to make us think that we are the most important person in the world. But becoming a man is knowing when to pay attention and when to give attention.

days.title: I Am Not The Messiah
days.scripture: JOHN 3:22-36 Then Jesus and his disciples left Jerusalem and went into the Judean countryside. Jesus spent some time with them there, baptizing people. At this time John the Baptist was baptizing at Aenon, near Salim, because there was plenty of water there; and people kept coming to him for baptism. (This was before John was thrown into prison.) A debate broke out between John’s disciples and a certain Jew over ceremonial cleansing. So John’s disciples came to him and said, “Rabbi, the man you met on the other side of the Jordan River, the one you identified as the Messiah, is also baptizing people. And everybody is going to him instead of coming to us.” John replied, “No one can receive anything unless God gives it from heaven. You yourselves know how plainly I told you, ‘I am not the Messiah. I am only here to prepare the way for him.’ It is the bridegroom who marries the bride, and the bridegroom’s friend is simply glad to stand with him and hear his vows. Therefore, I am filled with joy at his success. He must become greater and greater, and I must become less and less.
days.reflection: Lots of guys in the world today struggle with mental health issues. It is a confusing and stressful time to be alive. Sometimes people struggle with depression, anxiety, or fear. These can even be normal things that guys your age wrestle with. But there is another kind of psychological issue that people can wrestle with, and that’s people who are convinced that they are very, very important. They wrestle with what’s called a messiah complex. A messiah complex happens when someone overestimates their importance in the world. They act like they are the hero of humanity, and the most important person alive. Many successful men today seem to have traits of this. Elon Musk is trying To build a colony on Mars, Apple touts their latest products as “life-changing,” and Bill Gates is trying to build a better humanity with the Gates Foundation (named after himself of course ☺) Whether its sports, entertainment, or technology, some men seem to be convinced that they are more important than the rest of us. Many men in the world today seem to have a messiah complex. It’s so off-putting when you meet someone who thinks they are better than everyone else. No one likes the arrogant guy who thinks they are the smartest, coolest guy around. It’s important to have a proper view of yourself. And that’s what makes John the Baptist so remarkable. It's why you should seek to become a man like him. John the Baptist had the whole nation looking toward him. People loved his courage, charisma, and preaching. Many hoped he would be the one to save the Jewish people from the oppression of the Romans. But John knew who he was and, more importantly, who he wasn’t. Look at what he said when asked about his identity. You yourselves know how plainly I told you, ‘I am not the Messiah. I am only here to prepare the way for him.’ This is the perfect balance for a man. Confident in his calling, but without being an arrogant jerk. Knowing the role he was called to play, yet refusing to have a big head. Here is the truth: You don’t want to live like you have a messiah complex, because you aren’t qualified for the job. You didn’t create the world; you can’t save the world. But you can know the role you are meant to play and point others to Jesus. It’s enough to be a part of God's amazing team; you don’t have to be the star. The more you live with humility and point to the true messiah, the happier you will be. Say it out loud with John the Baptist: “I am not the Messiah.” It’s kind of a relief, not to think you are the savior of the world. Just enjoy being you.
questions: Of all the men you see in the media today, who seems to act like they have a messiah complex? Why?
What leaders in our world today do guys your age look to as heroes? Why?
Ask the person you are doing this study with if they ever struggled with pride when they were younger. If so, how did that manifest itself?

thoughts.verse: So humble yourselves under the mighty power of God, and at the right time he will lift you up in honor.
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to give you confidence. Confidence to know who he is, who you are, and how to act in a world of arrogance and insecurity.

days.scripture: JOHN 3:22-26 Then Jesus and his disciples left Jerusalem and went into the Judean countryside. Jesus spent some time with them there, baptizing people. At this time John the Baptist was baptizing at Aenon, near Salim, because there was plenty of water there; and people kept coming to him for baptism. (This was before John was thrown into prison.) A debate broke out between John’s disciples and a certain Jew over ceremonial cleansing. So John’s disciples came to him and said, “Rabbi, the man you met on the other side of the Jordan River, the one you identified as the Messiah, is also baptizing people. And everybody is going to him instead of coming to us.” John replied, “No one can receive anything unless God gives it from heaven. You yourselves know how plainly I told you, ‘I am not the Messiah. I am only here to prepare the way for him.’ It is the bridegroom who marries the bride, and the bridegroom’s friend is simply glad to stand with him and hear his vows. Therefore, I am filled with joy at his success. He must become greater and greater, and I must become less and less.
days.reflection: One of the biggest things that social media has done to impact teenagers is to get them thinking about “followers.” Influence for guys your age is often seen as more important than money, good looks, or even grades. In other generations, guys used to want to be firemen, or policemen, or in the marines. Today, they want to be Youtube stars. So often we are amazed or impressed when someone has 1 million followers or does something that goes viral. Having attention is what so many guys your age ache for. John the Baptist was a celebrity in his time. When he started baptizing the whole region came out to see him. They loved his preaching and respected his mission. But when Jesus came along, everyone left John and went over to Jesus. John's disciples were worried that he was losing influence. But John didn’t care because he knew he wasn’t at the center of the story. In John 3:30, he says, "He must increase, but I must decrease." These words are a powerful reminder of what it means to follow Christ. John knew that his mission was to prepare the way for Jesus and to point people to Him. He understood that his role was to decrease so that Jesus could increase in people's lives. This is a challenging concept for guys your age to grasp because you live in a world that values power, success, and fame. You are constantly bombarded with messages that tell us to be bigger, better, and stronger. But John's example reminds us that true greatness is found in serving others and putting Christ first. So, what does it mean for us to decrease so that Jesus can increase? Here are a few things to remember: Humility: John's words show that he recognized his own limitations and his need for a Savior. We too need to recognize our own weaknesses and shortcomings and turn to Christ for help. We should approach our daily lives with a posture of humility, recognizing that we are not the center of the universe, but rather small parts of God's grand plan. Service: John's ministry was all about serving others and preparing the way for Jesus. We should follow his example and seek to serve others in our daily lives. This means putting the needs of others before our own and using our gifts and talents to bless those around us. Surrender: John's words show that he was willing to let go of his own ambitions and desires in order to follow God's plan. We too should be willing to surrender our own plans and desires to God, trusting that His plans for us are far greater than anything we could imagine or hustle to work out for ourselves. Focus: John's entire ministry was focused on pointing people to Jesus. Signs never point to themselves; they point to something else. Our lives are not meant to be black holes that suck in all the attention; they are supposed to point beyond us to Jesus. It can be painful to lose influence if that’s what you think matters most. But for a believer, we don’t miss out on anything because we gain security in Jesus. We have his attention, and that’s better than going viral or having tons of followers. The more Jesus increases, the more your joy should. After all, he is the one all the signs are pointing to.
questions: It's easy to talk about “decreasing” and getting less attention, but it can be hard in real life. How do you tend to feel when people give their attention to someone else or you get overlooked? Can you think of a time this happened lately?
Can you think of 1 or 2 small little things you can do today to give attention to someone else and not try and take it for yourself?

Ask the person you are doing this study with to share some of the ways they have learned to find joy in decreasing so that Jesus can increase. Ask them to share why this is so important for a man to come to terms with.

thoughts.verse: “Don’t think you are better than you really are. Be honest in your evaluation of yourselves, measuring yourselves by the faith God has given us.
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to make you a secure young man who doesn’t need attention all the time. Ask him to give you joy in pointing beyond yourself to Jesus.

days.title: Behold The Lamb Of God
days.scripture: JOHN 1:29-31 The next day John saw Jesus coming toward him and said, “Look! The Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world! He is the one I was talking about when I said, ‘A man is coming after me who is far greater than I am, for he existed long before me.’ I did not recognize him as the Messiah, but I have been baptizing with water so that he might be revealed to Israel.”
days.reflection: You are living in the middle of a war. This war is happening right now, even as you read these words. It’s a war for your attention. William James, the father of modern psychology said, “we must reflect that, when we reach the end of our days, our life experience will equal what we have paid attention to, whether by choice or default.” John the Baptist was a man who cared what people paid attention to. That’s why he yelled out, “Look! The Lamb of God who takes away the sin of the world! First of all, let's break it down. The word "behold" means to look at or observe something with attention, and "Lamb of God" refers to Jesus Christ. So, when we say "Behold the Lamb of God", we're basically saying "look at Jesus". But why do we call Jesus the Lamb of God? Well, it goes back to the Bible, where Jesus is often compared to a lamb. In the Old Testament, lambs were often used as sacrifices to atone for the sins of the people. And in the New Testament, Jesus is described as the ultimate sacrifice for our sins. So, when we say "Behold the Lamb of God,” we're not just looking at Jesus as a person - we're looking at him as the savior of the world. It's a powerful statement that reminds us of the sacrifice that Jesus made for us on the cross. As a teenager, it can be hard to wrap your head around such a big concept. I mean, you’re still trying to figure out who you are and what your place is in the world. But I think that's what makes the phrase "Behold the Lamb of God" even more meaningful. It reminds us that there is something bigger than ourselves out there. It reminds us that we're not alone and that we have a purpose in life. And it reminds us that there is someone who loves us so much that he was willing to die for us. I think that's a pretty powerful message, don't you? It's easy to get caught up in your own little world, especially as a teenager. You worry about grades, friends, relationships, and all the other stuff that comes with this stage of life. But when we take a step back and look at the bigger picture, we realize that there's more to life than just us. Don’t just look at yourself. You will get selfish and sad. Don’t just look at the world You will be overwhelmed with all the bad stuff going on. Don’t just look at other people. They may let you down and disappoint you. Look at Jesus. He died to save you from your sin. He died to bring you to himself. God wants to use you to bring good news to others. People need to lift their vision. How can you help grab people’s attention so they can see who Jesus is and what he has done?
questions: Reflect on this quote again. “We must reflect that, when we reach the end of our days, our life experience will equal what we have paid attention to, whether by choice or default.” William James
What are 3 or 4 things that guys your age tend to pay attention to?
What are the top 3 or 4 things that you tend to pay attention to?
What do you find hard about paying attention to Jesus in your world today?
Ask your Dad how he learned to fight distractions and pay attention to Jesus each day.

thoughts.verse: We must pay the most careful attention, therefore, to what we have heard, so that we do not drift away.
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to thank Jesus for dying for your sins. Ask the Holy Spirit to help you fix your mind and attention on Jesus throughout your days.

days.title: The Bridegroom
days.scripture: JOHN 3:22-26 Then Jesus and his disciples left Jerusalem and went into the Judean countryside. Jesus spent some time with them there, baptizing people. At this time John the Baptist was baptizing at Aenon, near Salim, because there was plenty of water there; and people kept coming to him for baptism. (This was before John was thrown into prison.) A debate broke out between John’s disciples and a certain Jew over ceremonial cleansing. So John’s disciples came to him and said, “Rabbi, the man you met on the other side of the Jordan River, the one you identified as the Messiah, is also baptizing people. And everybody is going to him instead of coming to us.” John replied, “No one can receive anything unless God gives it from heaven. You yourselves know how plainly I told you, ‘I am not the Messiah. I am only here to prepare the way for him.’ It is the bridegroom who marries the bride, and the bridegroom’s friend is simply glad to stand with him and hear his vows. Therefore, I am filled with joy at his success. He must become greater and greater, and I must become less and less.
days.reflection: Have you ever been to a wedding? Have you ever been in a wedding? They are massive celebrations with guests, food, music, dancing, and a beautiful ceremony. The people in the wedding party add a lot to the wedding too. These are the friends of the bride and groom who have been planning and preparing to get things ready for the happy couple. Now can you imagine how weird it would be for the best man to try and steal all the attention for himself? If he butted in during the ceremony to talk about himself. If he cut in on the bride and started talking about how cool he was? Everyone would be mad. They would be disgusted at the immaturity and selfishness. The best man has one job: to get things ready for the main man, and not to take the attention for himself. John used the metaphor of a wedding to describe the relationship between himself and Jesus. He referred to Jesus as the bridegroom and himself as the friend of the bridegroom. In Jewish weddings of that time, the friend of the bridegroom was responsible for making sure that everything was prepared and ready for the wedding. He also acted as a helper between the bride and groom, ensuring that they were able to communicate with each other and make the plans they needed. In the same way, John saw his role as one of preparation and support for Jesus. He was overjoyed to hear Jesus' voice and to see Him fulfill His mission as the Messiah. John's joy was now complete because he had fulfilled his own mission and had played a part in ushering in the arrival of the Savior. He was content to be in the party and secure enough not to try and be the center of the party. This verse shows security and humility in John the Baptist in several ways. Firstly, John is acknowledging that he is not the central figure in this story. He refers to himself as the friend of the bridegroom, rather than the bridegroom himself. This shows that he understands his role as a servant of God and recognizes that his purpose is to prepare the way for Jesus, not to steal the spotlight or take credit for himself. Secondly, John shows humility by describing himself as someone who simply listens for the bridegroom's voice. He does not claim to have any special knowledge or insight that others do not have. Instead, he sees himself as a humble servant who is waiting and listening for the one who is greater than him. He was trying to get people to open their ears, but it was for Jesus’ message, not his own, Finally, John expresses his joy at hearing the bridegroom's voice. He does not take credit for anything that has been accomplished, but rather experiences complete joy in seeing the work of God being fulfilled through Jesus. The wedding was on, his work was done, and now the real party could begin. Don’t try and steal the central spot in life. Don’t try and make everything about you. Find your happiness in knowing you are loved by God, called for his purpose, and here to get people ready to know God’s forgiveness and love. Play your part, but don’t hog the spotlight.
questions: Ask your dad who the best man was at his wedding. Ask him to share a little bit about what that big day was like. How old was he? How long was he engaged? What were a couple of highlights? See if he has any photos he can share.
Have you ever been a part of a team that you loved playing on? In what way were you simply happy to be a part of it, without being at the center of it? How is this like our relationship with Jesus?
When you think about heaven, what is the first thought that comes to your mind? Now read Revelation
In what way is this different than what you thought about heaven and life with Jesus? Do you have any questions about it?

thoughts.verse: Haughtiness goes before destruction; humility precedes honor.
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you from it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment and ask God to give you a sense of joy at being on his kingdom team. Ask him for more vision and more joy for what’s ahead.

days.title: Finding Your Joy
days.scripture: JOHN 3:22-26 Then Jesus and his disciples left Jerusalem and went into the Judean countryside. Jesus spent some time with them there, baptizing people. At this time John the Baptist was baptizing at Aenon, near Salim, because there was plenty of water there; and people kept coming to him for baptism. (This was before John was thrown into prison.) A debate broke out between John’s disciples and a certain Jew over ceremonial cleansing. So John’s disciples came to him and said, “Rabbi, the man you met on the other side of the Jordan River, the one you identified as the Messiah, is also baptizing people. And everybody is going to him instead of coming to us.” John replied, “No one can receive anything unless God gives it from heaven. You yourselves know how plainly I told you, ‘I am not the Messiah. I am only here to prepare the way for him.’ It is the bridegroom who marries the bride, and the bridegroom’s friend is simply glad to stand with him and hear his vows. Therefore, I am filled with joy at his success. He must become greater and greater, and I must become less and less.
days.reflection: People love talking about themselves. Did you know that when you talk about yourself it activates the same part of the reward system of your brain as when you eat your favorite foods or engage in sexual behavior? Isn’t that wild? No wonder people can’t stop talking about themselves. One of the best things you can do to confront selfishness in your heart is to celebrate other people, but this can be really hard to do for a guy your age. No one likes to be in second place. No one likes to sit in the shade while someone else gets the light. No one wants to be Sam in Lord of the Rings or Robin in Batman and Robin. There is only one thing that can change the desire to put others before ourselves, and that’s love. Your parents put you before themselves every day. Your dad works hard to make sure you have the stuff you need. Your mom goes above and beyond to think of your needs before her own. And believe it or not, when you love someone, you actually find joy in seeing them succeed. It's important to remember that following Jesus is primarily about love. Not the mushy kind, but the powerful, deep, sacrificial kind of love. Therefore, I am filled with joy at his success. John the Baptist had joy because John the Baptist was full of love. Narcissistic individuals often view the success of others as a threat to their own self-esteem and may feel envious, resentful, or competitive. By contrast, a humble and empathetic person can take pleasure in the accomplishments of others, recognizing that their success does not diminish their own worth or achievements. The statement "Therefore, I am filled with joy at his success" reflects the humility and selflessness of John the Baptist. It shows that he is not envious or threatened by the success of Jesus but rather feels genuine happiness and fulfillment in witnessing the fulfillment of God's plan. This attitude stands in contrast to narcissism, which is characterized by a preoccupation with one's own achievements and a lack of empathy for others. Narcissistic individuals may feel threatened by the success of others and may be motivated by a desire for personal glory and recognition. By expressing his joy at Jesus' success, John the Baptist demonstrated a sense of humility and a willingness to put the needs and goals of others ahead of his own. He recognized his role as a messenger, and that his ultimate goal is to point others toward the greater good, even if it meant putting his own ambitions and desires aside. In this way, John the Baptist provides a powerful example of how humility and selflessness can lead to greater happiness and fulfillment, both for yourself and for others. By confronting narcissism and embracing humility, we can cultivate deeper connections with others and a greater sense of purpose and meaning in our lives. Boys want all the attention for themselves; men give it to Jesus. You are starting the journey of growing into a good and godly man. You are doing really, really well. Learn confidence and contentment and keep pushing ahead. The life of faith is a life of adventure. Way to go for walking another week down the Primal Path.
questions: Of all the stuff you get to do in your life, what are a couple of things that bring you the most joy?
Do you think there is a difference between joy and happiness? If so, what is it?
Ask your dad how he learned to find joy in seeing other people succeed. Ask him to tell a cool story about it.

thoughts.verse: Be happy with those who are happy, and weep with those who weep. Live in harmony with each other. Don’t be too proud to enjoy the company of ordinary people. And don’t think you know it all!
prompts: Is there something that stands out to you in it?
Is there a question you have about any of the words and what they mean?
Do you think you could explain what it means to someone else if they asked?

days.prayer: Take a moment to ask God to give you joy in your relationship with God, and pray for joy in some of the areas where you are struggling.

extras: WEEK 6 MANSCHOOL ROAD TRIP MANSCHOOL REMINDER The recipe for fun is pretty simple when you’re raising boys: add to any activity an element of danger, stir in a little exploration, add a dash of destruction, and you’ve got yourself a winner. - John Eldredge OPTION 1. ACTIVITY: SOMETHING TO SAY It's so hard not to talk about ourselves. Do you want to know why? Here is some psychological research on what happens in our brains when we talk about ourselves. Studies show that, hands down, our favorite topic of communication is, you guessed it, ourselves. As research reported in the Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences, study subjects showed signs of activity in the areas of the brain that are linked to value and motivation when they talked about themselves. Talking about oneself activates the same areas of the brain that light up when eating good food, taking drugs, and even having sex. Simply put, self-disclosure is gratifying. This week, have dinner with your family as normal but make it a challenge for your son to never talk about himself. The goal is to only ask questions and listen to the other members of your family, Adjust for any family dynamics, then take him out after for desserts to talk about what he learned about listening to others, prioritizing them, and giving them the attention. OPTION 2: WATCH Zoolander A classic film on how ridiculous narcissism is. Just some fun if you have the time. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MaEeSJZYkpY
FIELD NOTES PRAYER FOR MY SON
